era_context: The Roman Empire under Antoninus Pius is in the penultimate year of his reign (138–161 CE). The Eastern Han Dynasty under Emperor Huan continues its decline. The Kushan Empire is at its height under Kanishka II or his successor. This is a period of relative stability in the Mediterranean world, though the Antonine Plague will emerge within a decade.

events.title: Antoninus Pius reigns; frontier stability maintained
events.description: The year 158 CE falls within the reign of Antoninus Pius, a period characterized by relative peace and the maintenance of frontier stability. No major military campaigns, natural disasters, or political upheavals are recorded for this specific year. The emperor continued the policies of his predecessor Hadrian, focusing on consolidation and the defense of existing borders rather than expansion. The Antonine Wall in Britain, begun in 142 CE, was completed or nearing completion during this period.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from the Antonine Wall

events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for 158 CE. The characterization is based on the broader pattern of Antoninus Pius's reign.

events.title: Eastern Han Dynasty: Eunuch dominance and bureaucratic decay
events.description: Emperor Huan's reign continued to be characterized by eunuch dominance and the suppression of scholar-official factions. The year 158 CE saw no recorded major events, but the structural decay of the Han state persisted. The balance of power had shifted decisively toward the imperial harem and eunuchs, weakening the Confucian bureaucracy and central authority.

sources.name: Hou Han Shu (History of the Later Han)

events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for 158 CE.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The absence of recorded events reflects the bias of surviving sources toward recording crises rather than routine administration.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 158 CE is sparse. The surviving sources provide little detail for individual years during the reign of Antoninus Pius. The bias toward recording crises and major events means that years of relative stability are underrepresented.

graph_edges.note: Antoninus Pius's reign ends in 161 CE
